items.title: Criminal Jurisdiction Act 1802
items.summary: The Criminal Jurisdiction Act 1802 allows prosecution in England's King's Bench (now King's Bench Division) of UK public servants (civil or military) for crimes committed abroad in the course of their duties, treating such offences as if committed in England (laid in Middlesex), with punishments as for domestic crimes and potential disqualification from public office.
items.reason: Deleting this would leave UK public officials unaccountable for crimes committed abroad under color of office, enabling impunity, corruption, and abuse of power that erodes trust in government institutions, harms national interests, and distorts incentives for honest service; without it, local foreign courts may lack jurisdiction or will to prosecute, failing to achieve accountability that protects citizens' liberty from unchecked state power.
